items.headline: Study session on zoning and parkland protections
items.summary: City Council will review existing zoning designations and corrections, along with state law protections for recreational facilities and parkland sites. The agenda does not specify what changes or issues prompted the session.

items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Building permits issued for two 2-story homes at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Dr, totaling over $1 million in residential construction. This appears to be a small infill development on what may be a subdivided lot.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank receives permit for new Lytton Avenue location
items.summary: The bank is permitted to install illuminated signage at 250 Lytton Avenue, indicating a new branch opening in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: City to study eliminating chemical pesticides on public property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review a policy proposal to stop using chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased land. This would affect maintenance of parks, facilities, and landscaping across Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will examine the city's use of artificial turf, likely weighing environmental and maintenance tradeoffs for parks and sports facilities.
